title: Living in Boulder vlog 9 -  6 - 2017: Trying to get my Garmin computer to work.

enrichment.summary: Kyle vlogs about his struggles setting up Garmin cycling equipment — a broken speedometer, malfunctioning power meter pedals, and multiple tech support calls. He also mentions plans to create watt/cadence charts for his rollers and bike, and teases a large project making Strava segment screen-capture videos for the Boulder area.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: Believes his watt readings are inaccurate and much too low compared to a riding buddy's data
Struggles to stay motivated to ride without reliable watt data
Excited and committed — plans to upload thousands of segment screen captures to YouTube
Has a large backlog of high-quality Google Earth material he is eager to publish

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I went on a ride with a YouTube subscriber visiting the area.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I had to turn around during the ride because my wife called me and I got sick.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I've been busy setting up my bike with Garmin devices like the Carrey monitor, lot pedals, sputometer, and keys.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I spent 40 minutes on the phone with Garmin support.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: My sputometer didn't work yesterday, so I got new batteries, but it still didn't work today.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Garmin is sending me a new sputometer, which should arrive in three days.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: My lot meter doesn't work because it showed inconsistent wattage compared to another rider.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I tried updating my pedals but ran into issues with Garmin Express.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I plan to call Garmin support again tomorrow to resolve the pedal update issue.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I want to create charts for pedaling cadence, speed, and watts using data from my rides.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I made a chart based on data from the Quartzinger German Quartzinger website, but it's designed for a time trial bike, which I don't have.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I need to make another chart tailored to my bike.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I want to create a chart for my rollers to know exactly how many watts I'm using for each cadence.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I called Garmin support, and they suggested updating the software on my pedals.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm frustrated with my computer and microphone not working properly.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I plan to call the manufacturers of my devices to troubleshoot the issues.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I lack motivation to ride my bike unless I have my wattage data working.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I estimate my FTP (Functional Threshold Power) to be about 235 watts.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I haven't done a 28k time trial in a while but plan to do one soon.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm working on Strava screen captures and videos of every Strava segment in the Boulder area.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I plan to upload these Strava videos to my YouTube channel, Kyle Towns Google or YouTube channel.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I aim to have thousands of videos on my YouTube channel, covering various topics like ancient world villages and catastrophes.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I have a lot of content planned for my YouTube channel, including stuff I haven't even thought about yet.

transcript.text: Hello, Living in Boulder Vlog. It is for September 6th. It's 8.30. I can't remember when the last time I did a video was because I've been working. So I think it was like Saturday or something.
So on Sunday I went on a ride with my YouTube subscriber Tyne. He was visiting the area and we rode up to the top of Lee Hill. We were going to do a big ride up to 60 mile loop.
But he was ahead of me and then I zoomed ahead of him right before the top and then he had to turn around because his wife called him. He got sick.
So that was my one outside ride for the year. I've been busy trying to set up my bike. I got all these different Garmin things.
I have the heart rate monitor, the lot pedals, the speedometer, and the cadence.
And so I set that stuff up yesterday. I was on the phone with Garmin for like 40 minutes. They were trying to set that stuff up.
But my speedometer wouldn't work yesterday so I went and got new batteries and then it wouldn't work today.
So they're going to send me a new one. And he said it would only take three days to get here so that's cool.
And my wattmeter doesn't work. I'm pretty sure.
Because I went riding up the top of Lee Hill and Tyne and I, we weigh the same.
Our bikes weigh the same.
And we rode up pretty much together.
Got to the top at the same time.
And his watt's 235 for like 35 minutes of climbing.
But he was cranking a lot more than I was.
Which probably made him use more watts for how fast he was going.
But I, my thing said 150.
And it just doesn't make sense because I would ride really fast, really hard and I would say 100.
So the guy over at Garmin said I needed to update the, the pedals themselves.
But I plugged in and I tried to do that with the pedal program and it said I needed to turn off Garmin Express, the other Garmin program.
And it was already turned off.
So, but I was passing out so I took a nap when I got up.
It was too late to call him again.
So, I'll call him tomorrow and I'll see if I can get it figured out so, um, figured out where, um, I can, uh, update my pedals.
Uh, and then, uh, because I want to make these charts.
The reason I have been uploading videos is because I was making these charts for pedaling.
Um, pedaling cadence and speed and watts for, um, all the different gears that I have on my particular bike and I put it on my website.
I got it off this website where you click and then it tells you the watt speed and all that information because it had it from, like, all the different gears you could have.
But I have, you know, just one gear set.
So, I made a chart that you could look at right there.

Um, but it doesn't really mean that much to me because, um, I, that chart was made from this Kreuzinger, German Kreuzinger website where you figure it out, um, you know, with calculations on the computer.
And, uh, it was designed for a time trial bike and I don't have a time trial bike.
Um, so maybe I'd have to make another one for my bike.
I haven't even thought about that.
That'd be kind of complicated.
I don't even know how I would do that.
But before that, I want to make one for my rollers so that I know exactly how many watts I'm using for each cadence and each gear on my rollers.
But, uh, I can't do that unless my watt machine actually works.
Otherwise, I'm going to be putting false numbers in.
I called the first tech support guy and he's like, no way, it works, it works.
And I called the guy today and he's like, oh yeah, well, maybe you should update the, uh, software on the pedals.
The other guys didn't even say that you could do that.
So, hopefully this, uh, microphone is working because my computer's being really loud, like, right now, cooling itself.
And I'm not so sure I got this thing working yet.
So, if there's still noise, I think I'm going to call the people who make these and ask them why it's not working.
Um, so that's basically what I'm doing.
Just working on, like, so many different things that's making my head spin.
That's why I wasn't really riding my bike.
Plus, I don't have the motivation to ride my bike.
If I don't have the watts.
This is the same thing I've been trying to work on for, like, months since I decided I was going to start riding my bike.
So, um, that's what I'm doing tomorrow.
I'm going to call Garmin.
I'm going to see if I can get my pedals updated.
And I'm going to do a watt check.
Hopefully the watts are at least in the ballpark.
Because I was only, like, 60% of the watts of time when I went riding with.
And, uh, I know that I don't hammer at 150 watts.
That's ridiculous.
Um, so anyway, I pretty much know what my FTP is.
It's about a 235.
It's at 190 pounds.
So, I don't know how fast that is.
I haven't done the calculation on my website.
And I haven't done a time trial, a 20K time trial for a while.
But I'll do that pretty soon.
Just as soon as I get my watt thing working on my computer.
Um, and then I'll get going on the, uh, the Strava screen captures.
I'm making Strava videos of every single Strava segment in the Boulder area.
And then I'm going to put it up on its own YouTube channel.
On my Kyle Pounds Google Earth YouTube channel.
And that's going to motivate me to, uh, go ahead and do all the other Google Earth stuff for YouTube.
I'll have, like, thousands of videos, man.
I might have more videos.
Within a week or two, I could have more videos.
Or a couple months, I could have more videos on my Google Earth, Kyle Pounds Google Earth channel, than on my channel.
I could have, like, 4,000 videos or something.
And they're all really good Google Earth stuff.
I have, like, Rome, Boulder.
Rome from all the different epochs.
Boulder from all the different ages.
Um, ancient world villages.
Like, catastrophes.
Uh, everything.
Everything, everything, everything.
Even stuff I haven't thought about.
All the bike racer stuff that I can't, I don't even want to describe right now.
Groovy.
Okay, enough talking.
Peace out.
Oh my god, six minutes.
